a11y.usdApproxToggle: Toggle approximate US dollar amounts converted from yuan (not a live rate)

terminology.sectionLead: These terms use fixed translations so Chinese and English stay aligned.
terminology.defGuide: A travel guide here is a reusable itinerary with budgets and tips—meant to be checked, reused, and shared. It is not a generic “article” or marketing fluff.
terminology.defSubmit: Submit means sending us an original trip write-up for possible publication. Some content may stay on your device first, then enter review.
terminology.defReview: Review (moderation) is our check for policy, accuracy, and quality before content is shown to everyone—not “audit” (finance sense).

commerce.membershipSub: Optional support for indie ops—core features stay free; premium unlocks over time. No paid rankings; growth is useful content and shareable trip pages.
commerce.benefit1: Membership: free baseline; premium may add offline map packs, unlimited AI itineraries, and exclusive local audio guides.
commerce.benefit2: Affiliate bookings: commissions only when a trip is booked (e.g. Booking, Agoda, Skyscanner)—surfaced as saving tips, not banner ads.
commerce.benefit3: Merch: travel journals, compact toiletry kits, and other nomad-friendly goods—when offered.
commerce.benefit4: No paid search rankings—we protect product and community tone.
commerce.membershipNote: External signup opens in a new tab when configured; otherwise use About or the message board.
commerce.disclosure: Guides-first—not a mega-OTA or paid rankings. Membership, affiliate bookings, and merch are restrained options to keep the site running; details on each page.

home.globalMapSearchPlaceholder: Search by country, city, or keywords (e.g. Japan, Tokyo, cheap stay, sunset spot)
home.mapExploreLead: Search and pick a category below; tap a dot for tips and links to destinations or routes. AI assistant is also at the bottom-right.
home.mapCardSavingBlue: Use free-entry windows and walking viewpoints, budget about ¥0-80
home.mapCardSavingGreen: Choose local budget eats/night markets, budget about ¥30-120
home.mapCardSavingGreenHardCap: Local food + market combo, keep daily spend within ¥200
home.mapCardSavingYellow: Prioritize hostel beds and hotel deals, budget about ¥60-200
home.mapCardHighlightsBlue: View decks, sunset spots, scenic streets
home.mapCardTransportGreen: Direct bus to market, walk back
home.mapCardPitfallBlue: Avoid peak-time queues and add-on overpricing
home.mapCardPitfallGreen: Watch tourist-menu markups; check local ratings first
home.mapCardPitfallYellow: Confirm taxes, deposit, and cancellation terms
home.mapCardPitfallDefault: Skip overpriced viral spots; prioritize proven local routes
home.routePlanTransportNear: Prefer intercity bus/slow rail + local metro/bus, avoid taxis
home.routePlanTransportFar: Use low-cost flight/night bus across regions, then local metro/bus, avoid airport taxis
home.routePlanSafety1: Avoid isolated transfer points at night; travel with company when possible
home.routePlanSafety2: Split documents and cash; beware pickpockets in crowded areas
home.routePlanSafety3: Check visa and entry documents before cross-border segments
home.mobileLiteHint: Mobile lite mode: focus on map + search first, expand route planning when needed.
home.heroRegionAria: Home hero: diverse young travelers outdoors
home.heroTitleNew: Budget is limited, the world is not
home.heroLeadNew: Open the map, locate instantly, and save while moving. Spend less on waste, more on real experiences.
home.heroTitle: Spend less, feel more—independent trips, grounded info
home.heroLead: Real budgets and checkable routes—no mega-OTA shelf. Map + structured itineraries are rolling out; today use search, guides, and the AI assistant.
home.heroImageAlt: Young people of diverse backgrounds in casual activewear outdoors, laughing together—an energetic group ready to head out.
home.sloganRationale: We don’t optimize for everyone—no mega-booking shell. For travelers who plan a bit and spend on experiences, we keep copy lean and references checkable & shareable.
home.pillarBudgetBody: Verifiable costs—put money into memories, not waste.
home.pillarQuickBody: App-simple entry points and filters—fewer layers and outbound interruptions.
home.pillarHealthBody: Pace, altitude, food—travel that keeps you well.
home.sloganAlt3En: Budget is Limited, Dreams are Not.
home.sloganAlt3Zh: Your budget has limits, your dreams do not.
home.sloganAlt4Zh: Travel as a true global citizen.

home.roadbookLead: Draft an actionable navigation path—times, costs, map-ready stops, transfers—not a generic mega-site list.
home.roadbookNotesPlaceholder: e.g. easy pace, great food, fewer tourist crowds…
home.roadbookRegionUnspecified: No region specified—propose a suitable region from budget & days and briefly justify.
home.roadbookHint: Opens the assistant with a structured request. No embedded map yet—paste “city + keyword” into maps to verify; https links in replies are clickable.
home.roadbookDisabled: Roadbook needs the AI assistant enabled.
home.roadbookAiPrompt:
You are the Light Roam roadbook copilot. Produce an actionable navigation-path draft (time-of-day, cost bands, leg-to-leg transfers)—not a generic recommendation list.

User constraints (from the UI):
- Budget & trip length: {{budget}}; {{days}}
- Region / direction preference: {{region}}
- Extra notes: {{notes}}

Structure your answer as:
1) Daily timeline: headings “Day 1”, “Day 2”, …; split each day into morning/afternoon/evening where possible; each block states activity type and a per-person price band; food to a scannable block or dish class; lodging to a tier (hostel dorm / budget hotel).
2) For each key stop, add one line “City + place keywords” that is easy to paste into Google / Apple / local map search on a phone.
3) Transfers: between consecutive stops specify mode (metro line, bus, walk distance, ride-hail fare band); for inter-city legs give order-of-magnitude time and cautions.
4) Finish with a “To verify” section: tickets, opening hours, visa, safety/seasonality the user must check.

If information is missing, state reasonable assumptions and label them “Assumption”. Write clearly.
home.searchFormAria: Search guides by place and filters
home.joinCommunityDesc: Q&A, buddies, board—share snippets so the next traveler saves time and money.
home.roadmapLead: What we’re building—nothing is claimed as shipped before it exists.
home.roadmapMap: Destination pages embed OSM preview + illustrative path; global multi-destination routing still iterating.
home.roadmapItinerary: AI roadbook: home generator prompts for actionable paths & map-ready stop lines; embedded map & system navigation deep links iterating.
home.roadmapHonest: Safety and prices—always verify with official sources and the guide body.

seo.title: Roamwise · Light Travel — Youth-led budget travel · Real global routes
seo.description: A youth-focused budget travel site built on instant map positioning, saving-first route paths, and community support—real costs, executable guides, bilingual UX.
seo.keywords: Roamwise,Light Travel,budget travel,independent travel,low spend,map positioning,saving routes,travel guides,community support,AI trip plan,backpacking
seo.descHome: Start from map-first discovery: locate destinations fast, filter executable saving routes by budget and days, then continue with Q&A, buddies, and board support.
seo.descDestinations: Browse budget destinations by continent, tags, and search—daily budget hints, visa notes, and route counts.
seo.descArticles: Route list focused on actionable savings: narrow by budget, trip length, and destination to find low-cost paths you can execute right away.
seo.descArticleDetail: Route detail combines budget card, route flow card, risk checks, and guide body so you can move from browsing to execution with confidence.
seo.articleDetailMetaLead: {{destination}} route · about ¥{{budget}} per traveler · {{days}} days · saving path and execution notes
seo.descDestinationDetail: Destination overview: costs, visa, customs, risks, and related guides for this place.
seo.descFavorites: Your saved budget guides—quick access to routes and costs you care about.
seo.descCommunity: Community hub: Q&A, find buddies, and boards—travelers helping travelers.
seo.descCommunityQA: Travel Q&A—visas, routes, and money-saving tips. Content is stored locally for now.
seo.descCommunityQADetail: Q&A thread with the question and answers summary for trip planning.
seo.descCommunityBuddies: Find travel buddies—post your plan and needs; data stored locally for now.
seo.descBoard: Message board—share stories and warnings; follow community guidelines; local storage.
seo.descMembership: Optional support for indie operations—perks as stated, no mega-OTA promises.
seo.descBudgetCalculator: Budget calculator: quickly estimate total trip cost and per-person spend by days, travelers, and key expense categories.
seo.descAbout: About Roamwise—youth-led budget travel, light commercial model, content and share-first growth; community guidelines and glossary.
seo.descPrivacy: Privacy policy: what we collect, how we use data, and your choices.
seo.descTerms: Terms of service: rules and responsibilities for using this site.
seo.descFallback: Roamwise Light Travel—guides, destinations, and community.

footer.missionShort: Youth-led budget travel · Real routes · App-light UX
footer.taglineSub: Tech-led, content-first—optional support, not a mega-marketplace.

about.title: About this site
about.lightRoamSloganWallet: Slogan: the world is big, the wallet is small—don’t skip the joy.
about.lightRoamPositioning: Positioning: a global budget / light-travel AI navigator for young people—shipping iteratively.
about.lightRoamValues: Values: real > perfect; experience > checklist; independent > herd.
about.businessModelPhilosophy: We don’t buy paid search rankings—tone and trust come first.
about.businessModelMembership: Membership: core features stay free; premium may unlock offline map packs, unlimited AI trip tailoring, and exclusive local audio guides—as actually shipped.
about.businessModelAffiliate: Affiliate: we earn only when a booking completes (e.g. Booking, Agoda, Skyscanner). In the product it shows up as saving tips—not as banner ads.
about.businessModelMerch: Nomad lifestyle goods: travel journals, compact toiletry kits, and other “spiritual” travel products—when we offer them.
about.roadmapSwipeNote: Swipe-to-browse (Tinder-style) + favorite routes: in design—not shipped on Home yet.
about.pillarsIntro: Five practical dimensions aligned with the Light Roam brief above.
about.introBody:
We serve independent travelers who plan a bit and spend on experiences—facts you can check, feelings from the road not from ads. Navigation stays app-simple with minimal fluff. Membership and ads are optional; growth is content and shareable trip pages.

Map layers, richer AI itineraries, and stronger ticketing links are in progress—we won’t claim shipped features early. We won’t outspend OTAs—we invest in depth and tooling.
about.playbookFinalBlurb: Roamwise means roaming wisely: smarter spending unlocks farther trips and better memories—not cutting corners on what matters.
about.guidelinesBody: No vulgar language, political attacks, or discrimination based on faith or religion. Violations will be removed. You’re welcome to share experiences, tips, or complaints on the message board and help keep this space friendly.
about.mediaDisclaimerBody: Destination hero images are checked against country/region so we avoid obvious wrong-landmark pairings; some in-article photos may still be mood stock. Always verify details on the ground.

legal.privacyLead: Welcome to Roamwise (“the Site”). We respect your privacy. This policy explains how we handle information related to browsing guides, community features, and the AI assistant (if enabled). By using the Site you agree to this policy; if you disagree, please stop using the Site.
legal.privacySection1Body: (1) Usage & device data such as pages viewed and browser type, to improve content and performance. (2) Information you submit in forms or boards (some may stay only in your browser—see each page). (3) Third-party sign-in & comments: if you use GitHub via Giscus, identity and content are governed by that platform. (4) AI assistant: your messages are sent to the configured backend to generate replies—do not enter passwords or full ID numbers.
legal.privacySection2Body: We may use local storage for language, favorites, drafts, etc. You can clear it in browser settings.
legal.privacySection3Body: We do not sell personal data. Data may be shared with hosts or analytics as needed to run the service, or when required by law. Comments on GitHub are public per GitHub rules.
legal.privacySection4Body: You may exercise rights available in your region (access, correction, deletion, etc.). Contact us via published channels or GitHub Issues. We may update this policy and will change the date above.
legal.termsLead: Please read these terms before using Roamwise. Continued use means you accept them.
legal.termsSection1Body: We provide budget travel content and community tools. Some features rely on third parties (e.g. GitHub). We strive for accuracy but information may be incomplete or outdated—verify visas, safety, and transport yourself before travel.
legal.termsSection2Body: You agree not to post unlawful, infringing, false, abusive, or harmful content, and to follow community guidelines and partner rules. We may remove or restrict content that violates these terms.
legal.termsSection3Body: Site design and compilation are protected. Rights in user submissions depend on notices you give and applicable law; you warrant you have rights to what you submit.
legal.termsSection4Body: To the fullest extent permitted by law, we are not liable for indirect or consequential damages from using or inability to use the Site. We are not responsible for outages caused by force majeure or third-party services.
legal.termsSection5Body: We may revise these terms; updates will be posted here. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.
